title: Reviewer checklist (fidelity, plain-language, terminology, neutrality, cultural, privacy)
context: Every description and translation must pass qualified human reviewer sign-off before it is considered done. Reviewers need a structured checklist that covers all required review dimensions so that sign-off is consistent, documented, and enforceable in CI. The checklist depends on the plain-language guide (guide-001) and the cultural-sensitivity guide (guide-002) being in place.
objective: Create a reviewer checklist template covering all required review dimensions: factual fidelity to sourced facts (every claim maps to a sourceRef, no invented content), plain-language conformance (reading-level target met or attested), terminology and proper-noun accuracy (glossary applied, no mangled names/dates/materials), neutrality (no editorialising on contested histories), cultural appropriateness (sensitivity triage confirmed; no ungated sensitive-heritage or contested-legal content), privacy and defamation (no sensitive personal data, no unverified statements about living people), and agent-flag resolution (all UNCERTAIN flags resolved or accepted-with-reasoning).
acceptanceCriteria: Covers all required dimensions: factual fidelity, plain-language conformance, terminology/proper-noun accuracy, neutrality, cultural appropriateness, privacy/defamation, and agent-flag resolution
For translations specifically, adds a bilingual fidelity dimension: meaning preserved, proper nouns not translated, plain-ness preserved in the target language
Includes a 'reviewer independence' declaration field: reviewer is not the sole drafter and has declared COI
Each checklist item is a concrete yes/no or pass/fail question, not a vague aspiration
Is consistent with the review dimensions described in PLAN.md §8 and references guide-001 and guide-002
Designed to be used with the reviewer-handoff template (review-002) and recorded in review.yaml
output: templates/reviewer-checklist.md — structured checklist for domain-aware and bilingual reviewer sign-off
